get.description: Retrieves a single Transaction resource along with its details, including amount, origin, destination, and status.

200.description: A single Transaction is returned

id.description: An index, distinct from a Transaction's uid, that indicates the order in which the Transactions were created.

adjustment_uid.description: The UID of the Adjustment this Transaction is associated with, if any.

customer_uid.description: The unique id of the Customer associated with the Transaction.

created_at.description: The date and time when the Transaction is first recognized by Newline. This may be when the Transfer is initiated if the Transaction is associated with one.

custodial_account_uids.description: A list of unique ids referring to Custodial Accounts that are currently involved in this Transaction. An empty array will be returned if there are no Custodial Accounts involved. The array may contain more values as the Transaction progresses and will no longer grow when its status becomes settled. The array may still be empty by the time the Transaction is settled if there is no custodial asset movement as a result of this Transaction.

description.description: Description provided to the Transaction during origination
description.example: Transfer from "Clothing Branding installation" to "deposit architect hack"

destination_synthetic_account_uid.description: Synthetic Account where the asset has landed

initial_action_at.description: The date and time of some action (e.g. a debit authorization) that resulted in this Transaction's creation. When relevant and available, this value is a timestamp received by and generated externally to Newline; otherwise, this value will be when a transaction request was received and virtually identical to `created_at`

net_asset.description: Indicates whether the Customer's asset has gone up (`positive`), gone down (`negative`) or stayed the same (`neutral`) as a result of this Transaction.

return_uid.description: The uid of a related Return, when applicable.

settled_at.description: The date and time when the Transaction is settled

settled_index.description: Another unique index that indicates the order in which the Transactions were settled. Null if the Transaction's status is queued or pending.

source_synthetic_account_uid.description: Synthetic Account from where the asset is pulled

statement_posted_at.description: The date and time when the statement was posted

status.description:
When a Transfer is created via [POST /transfers](/reference/post_transfers), an associated Transaction is created with status `queued`. If it involves at least one custodial transfer, once the custodial transfer is initiated, the status will transition to `pending`.

Once a Transaction is settled, whether it has asset movement in custodial level, or is synthetic-only, or is RDFI (Receiving Depository Financial Institution, meaning no Newline Transfer was associated), it will have a `settled` status.

transaction_batch_uid.description: The unique id of the Transaction Batch this Transaction is associated with, if any.

transaction_event_uids.description: A list of unique ids referring to Transaction Events belonging to this Transaction. An empty array will be returned if there is no event involved. The array may contain more values as the Transaction progresses and will no longer grow when its status becomes settled. The array may still be empty at the time the Transaction is settled if there is no custodial asset movement as a result of this Transaction.

transfer_uid.description: The unique id of the Transfer this Transaction is associated with, if any.

type.description: Transactions are categorized by Type.  See the Transaction Statuses and Transaction Types section below for a list and definition of each Transaction Type that Newline supports.

us_dollar_amount.description: The amount of the Transaction in question. The amount will never be negative, please refer to the net_asset field for direction (i.e., credit or debit).

virtual_reference_number_uid.description: The VRN uid, when the transaction was processed using a VRN.

instant_payment.description: Instant payment information. Only present if the Transaction is an instant payment.

network_transaction_id.description: For newline initiated instant payments, a unique newline trace ID assigned to the transaction. For externally initiated instant payments, a unique transaction ID assigned by the network facilitating the instant payment.

network.description: The network on which the instant payment was executed

failure_code.description: A code indicating failure reason. It will only appear if transaction status is `failed`

failure_reason.description: A description of why the transaction failed. It will only appear if transaction status is `failed`

counterparty_name.description: Name of the business or individual who owns the counterparty Account.

counterparty_bank_name.description: Name of the financial institution where the counterparty account is held.

counterparty_bank_routing_number.description: The routing number associated with the counterparty bank account.

counterparty_account_number_last_four.description: Last four digits of counterparty account number

payment_id.description: An originator-defined value. For RTP transactions, this will be the End-to-End ID.

original_end_to_end_id.description: This field is currently always null.

transmitter_name.description: Name of the initiating counterparty. Deprecated in favor of `initiating_party_name`.

instant_payment_transmitter.description: Name and address of the Transmitter.

counterparty_bank_address.description: Address of the financial institution where the external account is held.

counterparty_address.description: Address of the business or individual who owns the external account.

wire.description: Wire-specific information. Only present if the Transaction is a wire.

counterparty_address.description: Address of the business or individual who owns the external account.

line1.description: Part of an unparsed address. Data may vary or be null.

line2.description: Part of an unparsed address. Data may vary or be null.

line3.description: Part of an unparsed address. Data may vary or be null.

counterparty_name.description: Name of the business or individual who owns the counterparty Account.

counterparty_bank_address.description: Address of the financial institution where the external account is held.

counterparty_bank_name.description: Name of the financial institution where the counterparty account is held.

counterparty_bank_routing_number.description: The routing number associated with the counterparty bank account.

intermediary_bank_address.description: Address of the intermediary bank. To be populated if an intermediary bank is required to execute the wire transfer.

intermediary_bank_name.description: Name of the intermediary bank, when applicable. For wires only. Maximum 35 characters.

intermediary_bank_routing_number.description: The ABA routing number associated with the intermediary bank involved in the wire transfer. This value is required if intermediary_bank_name or intermediary_bank_address is present; optional otherwise

originator_account_number_last_four.description: Last four digits of originator account number.

ach.description: ACH-specific information. Only present if the Transaction is an ACH. The optional `iat` object within `ach` contains details for International ACH Transactions. It is only included if `iat_ach_display_enabled` is set to `true` for the program and the transaction contains IAT data.

ach_return_code.description: ACH return code, pulled from the Return File

ach_return_reason.description: ACH return reason, pulled from the Return File

noc_code.description: Notification of Change (NOC) code, returned from the RDFI (Receiving Depository Financial Institution).

noc_corrected_data.description: Notification of Change (NOC) corrected data description, returned from the RDFI (Receiving Depository Financial Institution).

noc_reason.description: Notification of Change (NOC) description, returned from the RDFI (Receiving Depository Financial Institution).

originator_routing_number.description: Routing Number of individual or business originating the ACH transfer.

foreign_exchange_indicator.description:
Identifies if the currency of the transaction changes.
The Newline API supports the following values:

- `FF` - Fixed to Fixed
- `FV` - Fixed to Variable

foreign_exchange_reference_indicator.description:
Defines the type of value in the "foreign_exchange_reference" field.
The Newline API supports the following values:

- `"1"` - Foreign Exchange Rate
- `"2"` - Foreign Exchange Reference Number
- `"3"` - If Foreign Exchange Indicator is "FF"

foreign_exchange_reference.description: If "foreign_exchange_indicator" is "FF" then this field blank;  otherwise it will contain either the foreign exchange rate used to execute the foreign exchange conversion  or another reference to the foreign exchange transaction.

iso_destination_country_code.description: Country code of the destination country.

iso_originating_currency_code.description: Currency code for transaction origination currency.

iso_destination_currency_code.description: Currency code for transaction destination currency.

transaction_code.description:
Transaction code describing a debit or credit and the account type used in the payment.
Payment transaction code requirements differ based on the receiving country.

transaction_type_code.description:
Identifies the IAT Transaction Type Code.
The Newline API supports the following values:

- `"ANN"` (Annuity)
- `"RLS"` (Rent/Lease)
- `"POS"` (Point of Sale Entry)
- `"BUS"` (Business/Commercial)
- `"SAL"` (Salary/Payroll)
- `"RCK"` (Re-presented Check Entry)
- `"DEP"` (Deposit)
- `"TAX"` (Tax)
- `"SHR"` (Shared Network Transaction)
- `"LOA"` (Loan)
- `"TEL"` (Telephone-Initiated Entry)
- `"MIS"` (Miscellaneous)
- `"ARC"` (Accounts Receivable Entry)
- `"WEB"` (Internet-Initiated Entry)
- `"MOR"` (Mortgage)
- `"BOC"` (Back Office Conversion Entry)
- `"PEN"` (Pension)
- `"MTE"` (Machine Transfer Entry)
- `"REM"` (Remittance)
- `"POP"` (Point of Purchase Entry)

foreign_payment_amount.description: The value of the funds if the destination currency code is different from destination country code.

foreign_trace_number.description: Optional trace number that can be supplied by the originator.

originator_street_address.description:
The street address of the originator. On received IATs, 
this is the originator's street address supplied with the received payment.

originator_city_state_province.description:
The city and, if applicable, the state or province of the Originator. 
An asterisk (*) will separate the two values in this field.

originator_country_postal_code.description:
The two-character alphabetic country code, as defined within the ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 code list, 
and the postal code of the Originator. An asterisk (*) will separate the two values in this field.

odfi_identification_number_qualifier.description:
A 2-digit code that identifies the type of ID in the "odfi_id" field.
The Newline API supports the following values:

- `"01"` - National Clearing System Number
- `"02"` - BIC Code
- `"03"` - IBAN

odfi_id.description: The bank identification number of the DFI at which the Originator maintains their account.

rdfi_identification_number_qualifier.description:
A 2-digit code that identifies the type of ID in the "rdfi_id" field.
The Newline API supports the following values:

- `"01"` - National Clearing System Number
- `"02"` - BIC Code
- `"03"` - IBAN

rdfi_id.description: The bank identification number of the DFI at which the Receiver maintains his account.

rdfi_branch_country_code.description: Country code of the RDFI. Will always be US for domestic IAT transactions

counterparty_street_address.description: The street address of the Receiver.

counterparty_city_state_province.description:
The city and, if applicable, the state or province of the Receiver. 
An asterisk (*) will separate the two values in this field.

counterparty_country_postal_code.description:
The two-character alphabetic country code, as defined within the ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 code list, 
and the postal code of the Receiver. An asterisk (*) will separate the two values in this field.

payment_related_information_1.description:
The payment related information found in the remitance information addenda when provided. 
When the payment related information for an IAT Entry includes the identification of a country, 
that country must be identified using that country's two-character alphabetic country code, 
as defined within the ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 code list.

An asterisk ("*") will be used as the delimiter between the data elements in the field.
payment_related_information_1.example: NTE*ADD*FCS003348198932 PAYMENT-NUM  351380\NTE*ADD*601 POP GOODS AND SERVICES

payment_related_information_2.description:
The payment related information found in the remitance information addenda when provided. 
When the payment related information for an IAT Entry includes the identification of a country, 
that country must be identified using that country's two-character alphabetic country code, 
as defined within the ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 code list.

An asterisk ("*") will be used as the delimiter between the data elements in the field.

foreign_correspondent_banks.description: List of foreign correspondent banks involved in the IAT transaction.

name.description: The name of the Foreign Correspondent Bank when provided

id_number_qualifier.description:
A 2-digit code that identifies the type of ID in the "id_number" field when provided.
The Newline API supports the following values:

- `"01"` - National Clearing System Number
- `"02"` - BIC Code
- `"03"` - IBAN

id_number.description: The bank identification number of the Foreign Correspondent Bank when provided

branch_country_code.description: A two-character alphabetic country code to identify the country in which the branch of the Foreign Correspondent Bank is located when provided.

put.description: Approves or denies a pending Transaction. This endpoint is used to explicitly authorize or reject a Transaction before it is executed.

authorization_status.description: The current status of the transaction authorization.

denied_memo.description: Freeform text (up to 44 characters) that should be attached to the transaction if the transaction is to be denied. Optional.

denied_reason.description: The reason for the authorization denial. For wire transactions, must be one of (`vrn_archived`), (`vrn_locked`), (`international_payment`), (`unauthorized_credit`). For ACH transactions, must be one of (`vrn_archived`), (`vrn_locked`), (`debit_limit_exceeded`), (`unauthorized_credit`), (`unauthorized_debit`), (`insufficient_funds`)

200.description: The authorization resource is returned

authorization.description: Transaction Authorization information. Only present if authorization was required for a given transaction.

authorization_finalized_at.description: The date and time when authorization for this transaction got finalized. It will only appear if authorization status is in one of the final statuses: `auto_approved`, `auto_denied`, `client_approved`, `client_denied`, `client_unanswered_auto_approved` or `client_unanswered_auto_denied`.

authorization_required_by.description: The date and time representing the deadline by which the authorization decision should be made by client. If no authorization is provided by this date, the transaction status will transition to client_unanswered_auto_approved.

authorization_status.description: A value indicating the current state of authorization for this transaction.

denied_memo.description: Freeform text (up to 44 characters) attached as a memo to authorization. It will only appear if authorization status is `auto_denied`, `client_denied` or `client_unanswered_auto_denied`.

denied_reason.description: A description of why the authorization was denied. It will only appear if authorization status is `auto_denied`, `client_denied` or `client_unanswered_auto_denied`.

errors.title: Transaction cannot be authorized
errors.detail: Authorization status does not allow updates

errors.title: Transaction cannot be authorized
errors.detail: Transaction authorization window has passed

errors.title: Transaction cannot be authorized
errors.detail: Transaction is not eligible for authorization

403.description: Client authorization disabled. (The Program is not configured for Client Authorization)

errors.title: Client authorization is not enabled
errors.detail: This program is not configured for client authorization of transactions.

422.description: An exception occurred while authorizing transaction

errors.title: Transaction cannot be authorized
errors.detail: An exception occurred while authorizing transaction

get.description: Retrieves a list of Transaction Events. Transaction Events represent the steps required to complete a Transaction and can be used to track its progress or review its history.

schema.description: Filter by source Custodial Account. Multiple values are allowed, e.g. `source_custodial_account_uid[]=dmRtw1xkS9ghrntB&source_custodial_account_uid[]=Gw4gr1T81YrvLT6M`.
Filter by destination Custodial Account. Multiple values are allowed, e.g. `destination_custodial_account_uid[]=W55zKgvAk3zkpGM3&destination_custodial_account_uid[]=66gso857LyQWC6XN`.
Filter by Custodial Account, source or destination. Multiple values are allowed, e.g. `custodial_account_uid[]=dmRtw1xkS9ghrntB&custodial_account_uid[]=W55zKgvAk3zkpGM3`.
Filter by type. Multiple values are allowed, e.g. `type[]=odfi_ach_deposit&type[]=odfi_ach_withdrawal`.

parameters.name: transaction_uid
parameters.description: Filter by Transaction. Multiple values are allowed, e.g. `transaction_uid[]=SMwKC1osz77DTEiu&transaction_uid[]=f1UDvUVNnXTKtgxu`.

200.description: A list of Transaction Events is returned

data.description: Transfer from Bank ABC 123 to Newline 999

get.description: Retrieves a single Transaction Event resource, including its status, timestamps, and associated Transaction details.

200.description: A single Transaction Event is returned

uid.description: Newline-generated unique id resource specific to the current endpoint

settled_index.description: A unique index that indicates the order in which Transaction Events were settled

transaction_uids.description: A list of UIDS referring to Transactions this Transaction Event belongs to.

source_custodial_account_uid.description: Custodial Account from which the asset is pulled

destination_custodial_account_uid.description: Custodial Account where the asset lands

custodial_line_item_uids.description: A list of UIDS referring to Custodial Line Items belonging to this event. There are always at least two line items if the status is `settled`.

status.description: Transaction Events will always have a status of `settled`

us_dollar_amount.description: The amount will never be negative

debit_card_uid.description: If this Transaction Event represents a Debit Card purchase or refund, the UID of the Debit Card used for the purchase/refund. Otherwise not provided.

net_asset.description: Indicates whether the Customer's asset has gone up (`positive`), gone down (`negative`) or stayed the same (`neutral`) as a result of this Transaction Event. This value is determined by `type`.

description.example: Transfer from Bank ABC 123 to Newline 999

created_at.description: The date and time when the event is first recognized by Newline. This may be when the Transfer is initiated if the event is associated with one.

settled_at.description: The date and time when the event is settled

value.description: Transfer from Bank ABC 123 to Newline 999

post.description:
Initiates a Return of an ACH or wire payment.
A full ACH addenda is not available for ACH returns because an addenda record is used for the return itself; the `addenda_info` field contains the remaining available space.
For wire returns, the `wire_instruction` field is limited to 70 characters because Newline prefixes the instructions with the original wire transaction identifier (e.g., IMAD).

external_uid.description: Client supplied unique identifier. It must be unique within the customer. If the same value is given, no new resource will be created.

return_reason.description: Freeform text (up to 140 characters) documenting the reason for return. For wire returns, this value will be sent to the other bank.

ach_return_code.description: The reason an ACH payment is being returned. Required if returning an RDFI transaction. Optional if reversing an ODFI transaction.

addenda_info.description: Freeform text (up to 44 characters) to attach a memo to return.
